content.full_text:
January 17, 1991

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation earlier today regarding the interpretation of the phrase "cumulative amount" in Government Code Section 82018(b).

The question I posed relates to a municipal election to be held on February 26, 1991. A committee formed to oppose measures on the ballot in this election is required to file a semi-annual campaign statement covering the period through December 31, 1990 as well as a first pre-election campaign statement covering the period January 1, 1991 through January 12, 1991. Both campaign statements are due on January 17, 1991. I asked whether the committee is required to carry forward the cumulative amounts from the prior calendar year or whether new cumulatives should begin as of January 1, 1991.

You advised me that the cumulative amounts would be calculated as of January 1, 1991. Government Code Section 82018(b) would require the committee to carry forward the cumulative amounts from 1990 only if the period covered by the first pre-election campaign statement included part of the prior calendar year. Since this is not the case in this instance, the cumulative amounts begin on January 1, 1991.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard  
January 17, 1991  
Page 2

If I have misstated your advice, please let me know as soon as possible. I greatly appreciate your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Jerry Margaret Simmons

JMS/dm
